entry.title: Washington Post: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: European Commission backs Kyiv’s E.U. ambitions; eastern battles rage on
  • European Commission recommends E.U. candidate status for Ukraine
  • Mexico City trades colorful food stalls for drab uniformity
  • Top British official orders Julian Assange’s extradition to U.S.
  • U.N. turns to crowdfunding to salvage decaying oil tanker off Yemen
  • As nuclear test looms, North Korea reports intestinal epidemic
  • Third American missing in Ukraine identified as Marine veteran
  • China launches cutting-edge aircraft carrier, first to be locally designed
  • Colombia’s historic presidential election: What you need to know
  • The troubled paradox of U.S. democracy
  • Ukraine war pushes global displaced to record high, U.N. says
  • Alleged Russian spy studied at Johns Hopkins, won ICC internship
  • In Kyiv visit, European leaders back Ukraine’s candidacy to join E.U.
  • France, Germany, Italy back Ukraine’s E.U. candidacy in first wartime trip to Kyiv
  • Myanmar junta vows to execute pro-democracy activists
  • Burkina Faso massacre highlights a strengthening insurgency
  • Estonian leader urges faster help for Ukraine amid signs of war fatigue
  • Pakistan urges citizens to give up tea to help economy
  • Giant tortoise believed extinct confirmed alive in Galápagos Islands
  • Andrée Geulen, rescuer of Jewish children in Belgium, dies at 100
  • Greece, Cyprus won't follow Turkey in tension-raising talk
  • Leaders meet in Madrid to mark world day to fight drought
  • Palestinian reporter for Iranian agency beaten in West Bank
  • UK govt approves extradition of Assange; he plans to appeal
  • Belgian bishop declines cardinal honor over abuse record
  • Pride events targeted in surge of anti-LGBTQ threats, violence
  • These kids don’t have fathers. He takes them fishing every weekend.
  • Tears for the racist torment my father endured
  • Video shows inmates intervene after guard was choked with a pillowcase
  • A county approved Pride Month. In June, officials overturned the vote.
  • Children served floor sealant instead of milk at Alaska day care
  • Paul Molloy, co-founder of housing program for addicts, dies at 83
  • John Eastman says Ginni Thomas invited him to speak on ‘election litigation’
  • Florida fraud suspect tried to flee to Cuba on a Jet Ski, feds say
  • Charging 69 cents for gas was an ‘honest mistake’ he’s hoping to repay
  • Biden’s ‘energy whisperer’ balances climate agenda, rising gas prices
  • Congressional covid funding deal appears ‘dead’ after GOP criticism
  • Trump aides told him that using Pence to overturn election was illegal
  • Justice Dept. says Jan. 6 House panel hampering major Proud Boys trial
  • Jan. 6 hearing: Pro-Trump lawyer sought pardon after pushing plan to overturn election
  • Anti-vaccine doctor sentenced to prison for Jan. 6 trespassing
  • Top Workplaces 2022
  • How D.C.-area employers kept workers happy amid the Great Resignation
  • Four Top Workplaces chiefs offer lessons on crisis management
  • A constant state of flux: Settling into being unsettled at work
  • The fight to destigmatize mental health care in the defense industry
  • 7 ways to lower your credit card debt after the Fed rate hike
  • Here’s how the 2022 Top Workplaces were selected
  • Israel and Egypt sign gas export deal as Europe seeks Russia alternative
  • Stop Slacking me: How to set digital boundaries for chatty coworkers
  • This Styrofoam-eating ‘superworm’ could help solve the garbage crisis
  • Google’s AI passed a famous test — and showed how the test is broken
  • Audio: Musk answers staff questions at Twitter town hall
  • Severna Park, Md., waterfront estate on the market for $15.9 million
  • Rising from the ashes
  • The Fed Is Still Struggling to Get Its Story Straight
  • The BOJ Seems to Be Reveling in Obstinate Isolation
  • ECB Plays Fast and Loose With the Law in Bond Plan
  • Student Loans in the UK Are About to Get More Complicated
  • How a Battery Metals Squeeze Puts EV Future at Risk
  • What It Would Take for Jack Ma’s Ant to Reboot an IPO
  • Waive Pharma’s Vaccine Rights? What That Would Mean
  • Why Japan’s Yen Is the Weakest in 20 Years and What That Means
  • How the gun talks expose the myth of an obstructionist Senate
  • The ripple effect of the Ukraine war is ‘a potential mass starvation event’
  • It’s all over but the shouting
  • Baseball looks strange, but the weird thing is how easily it happened
  • The gun-violence plague is evolving, dangerously
  • Historically Black colleges should no longer have to do more with less
  • We warned Google that people might believe AI was sentient. Now it’s happening.
  • Juneteenth is meant to unite us, just like the Fourth of July
  • A young soldier’s death reminds Ukrainians that the war is taking their best
  • Hold Montgomery officials accountable for school closures
  • Donald Graham: Watergate resonated because The Post reported the truth
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 17 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-17 11:11AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • AFL 2022 Round 14 LIVE updates: Essendon Bombers jump to quarter-time lead over St Kilda Saints
  • As Russia presses assault, Ukraine given possible path to join EU
  • Gus on the Ciraldo meeting with the Wests Tigers: Six Tackles with Gus - Episode 15

  • UK orders extradition of Julian Assange to United States
  • Thrilling Cobbo dive stretches Broncos lead
  • NRL 2022 LIVE updates: Melbourne Storm v Brisbane Broncos

  • Cowboys storm home over Manly amid Origin mayhem

  • Hammer summoned for HIA after copping knee
  • Croker on fire against the Cowboys
  • Garrick and Harper combine for 90m try
  • Referee cancels Captains Challenge

  • CCC launches lobbying audit amid surge and ‘disproportionate’ access
  • Flagship new Brisbane bridge may not be finished before next election
  • Fine hike to hit disadvantaged Queenslanders hard

  • $760m extra for hospitals while health system fixes are found
  • Uber calls for industry-wide earnings safety net for gig workers
  • Boats are not the problem, it’s the 130,000 asylum seekers living here
  • Dumping asylum seekers in old killing fields: an Australian original
  • Turn with caution: Potholes ahead as PM tries to round climate corner
  • Power and inflation crises work for the new government but for how long?
  • Energy crisis boosts case for renewables, top policy adviser says
  • Albanese did not create the energy crisis, but he will have to fix it
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • Best of cartoons, June 16, 2022
  • Universal paid domestic violence leave to be minister’s first act
  • The energy crisis NSW didn’t need to endure
  • Detective took everything Guy Sebastian told him as ‘gospel’, court told
  • Corporate culture, not ‘bad apples’ to blame at Star, inquiry told
  • Detective denies ‘deliberate omissions’ in Dawson investigation, trial told
  • Behind the scenes: Carrie Fellner on her investigation into NSW regional hospitals
  • Anger at King’s School over plans to fly headmaster to prestigious British regatta
  • Two men arrested after alleged sexual assault of woman met via dating app
  • Leo’s houseful of treasures were rescued from the street - even his cat
  • Stalled industrial dispute adds $50 million to Sydney Metro costs
  • Energy crunch an unexpected boon for Mike Cannon-Brookes
  • Energy companies are holding all Australians to ransom
  • From the Archives 1972: Whoosh! Concorde’s first Sydney landing
  • Sogavare promises Wong no persistent Chinese ‘military presence’ in Solomons
  • New Zealand ditches COVID tests for inbound travellers
  • Ties off: traditional dress code ditched at Royal Ascot to cope with heatwave
  • Gina Rinehart, sanctioned by Putin, calls on Australia to ramp up defences
  • Beauty brand Revlon filed for bankruptcy
  • In Saudi LGBTQ crackdown, even rainbow toys aren’t safe
  • Caught Russian spy was on the way to Hague internship
  • Man shocked to find out he moved into murder home
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Plot foiled: Why the little man who wants to be a big tsar put me on his list
  • Can Prince Charles save the Commonwealth once the Queen is gone?
  • What are the Capitol riot hearings all about?
  • ‘Feeling the support’: Zelensky gets more weapons after EU leaders’ high-stakes visit
  • Lawyers told Trump that pressuring Pence to overthrow election was illegal
  • Russia has announced it's blacklisting more Australian citizens
  • Russia sanctions hundreds of Australians including journalists
  • Macron decries massacres, war crimes as European leaders visit Ukraine to support Zelensky
  • Eight-month-old boy died while mother on 21-hour drug-fuelled episode
  • Former Labor political adviser spared jail
  • Families of Kate Leadbetter and Matthew Field push for youth justice law reform
  • New details revealed about death of baby Queensland boy
  • Energy crisis should hasten push into renewables
  • Man killed after he stopped to help other Victorian motorist
  • NSW government to expand gambling tax
  • Girl allegedly stabbed by other children in Melbourne armed robbery
  • Two men charged after alleged gang rape
  • Lead cop in Lynette Dawson disappearance admits he did not take statements from key witnesses
  • Prime Minister Anthony Albanese hosts first national cabinet meeting
  • Heat, shoddy homes and unreliable power leave people weak in remote NT
  • William Tyrrell's foster parents allegedly 'kicked and hit girl with wooden spoon'
  • Corruption watchdog to deepen probe into influence of lobbyists on Palaszczuk government
  • ‘BNPL crunch’: Humm, Latitude walk away from sale
  • ‘This is an airport issue’: Alan Joyce defends Qantas amid fresh wave of anger
  • ‘Big change’: In Melbourne, a retail recovery is under way
  • Volatility is king: Markets brace for more turbulence amid meltdown
  • $3b deal hits another roadblock as Canadian giant reviews Link offer
  • Bitcoin ‘bank’ boss fighting for his reputation after triggering crash
  • ‘Significantly accelerated’: Bubs Australia upgrades guidance
  • ASX suffers worst week in two years as recession fears spook markets
  • Wall Street’s implosion is terrible news for Biden - and it’s going to get worse
  • Elon Musk flags job cuts in unorthodox meeting with Twitter staff
  • Diversity and inclusion aren’t the same, and both need your attention
  • Rural escape: Where talking about the weather is more than small talk
  • Am I thin-skinned, or is my colleague’s nagging getting nasty?
  • A Rolex, private jet and chips: How Star courted John Khoury to gamble in Queensland
  • ‘It’s coming’: Small businesses brace for power bill shock
  • Revlon, beauty icon in crowded market, files for bankruptcy
  • The variety and challenge of being a principal, ‘it’s all gold to me’
  • Ticking crypto time bomb could spark another financial crisis
  • ‘Orphaned inventions’: Life-saving AI discoveries trapped in legal black hole
  • An octopus cracks a cold case? Mystery and a magical fantasy books to read next
  • Stories of lost souls, reality TV and anonymous sex make for vivid reading
  • ‘I was just unemployed’: The unknown Australian filmmaker who is suddenly everywhere
  • Watch the trailer for Children's Hospital
  • ‘A f---ing good story’: Minnie Driver on Damon, Weinstein and being ‘difficult’
  • The best movies, TV, music, podcasts and books of the year (so far)
  • Forget girl bosses, I’m just here for the insane nanny
  • Here’s looking at you, Kid
  • Dating as a black woman in Australia? It’s an experience worth an award-winning podcast
  • Ana de Armas stuns as Marilyn Monroe in 'Blonde' trailer
  • Kathy Lette: ‘Impaling enemies on the end of your pen is so satisfying’
  • Game subscriptions heat up as PlayStation joins the race
  • Our spectacular energy failure: governments hobbled the market and now they must rescue it
  • The frogs have stopped calling where Perth wetlands have dried up
  • Once used to moor rowboats, the jetties on this Yanchep lake now sit 10 metres from the shore
  • National weather forecast for Friday June 17
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: NY Times: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • For Mike Pence, Jan. 6 Began Like Many Days. It Ended Like No Other.
  • Trump, Told It Was Illegal, Still Pressured Pence to Overturn His Loss
  • France and Germany Stand With Ukraine, and Putin Can Wait
  • Europe Offers Ukraine a Hope of Joining the E.U., but Not a Vast Arsenal
  • Inflation Expected to Remain High Even as Economy Slows and Layoffs Rise

  • Gun Talks Snag on Tricky Question: What Counts as a Boyfriend?

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 17 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-17 11:00AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Unlocking Students Full Potential
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong to ramp up testing amid surge in cases; infections top 1,000 for 3 days straight
  • Hong Kong consumption vouchers: what you need to know about second batch of e-coupons, who is eligible and special offers available
  • How Hong Kong’s ‘Covid-19 heroes’ rose to the challenges they faced
  • Soaring interest rates: Hong Kong’s new mortgage borrowers and highly leveraged buyers are most at risk in the property market
  • Hong Kong’s biggest health crisis in 25 years: why did city stumble over Covid-19? Experts say it was too slow to change tactics, inflexible, put politics first
  • US pressures Iran by targeting Hong Kong and UAE firms
  • Incoming Hong Kong leader John Lee relies mostly on incumbent officials to fill ranks of his governing team
  • Hong Kong courts propose remote hearings for certain criminal trials, punishments for recording or broadcasting sessions
  • Veteran Hong Kong lawmaker Regina Ip set to become convenor of incoming leader John Lee’s de facto cabinet, sources say
  • Climate Change: Greater Bay Area holds the key to Hong Kong’s climate goals, delegates say at SCMP’s inaugural environmental summit
  • Suspects started clash between residents and protesters that led to elderly cleaner’s death, injured witness tells Hong Kong court
  • Company owned by Hong Kong justice chief Teresa Cheng’s husband sued for price fixing over air-conditioning works potentially worth HK$2 billion
  • Tangshan restaurant attack suspect was wanted by Chinese authorities over previous crimes
  • On Taiwan’s frontline islands ‘life goes on’ as jets scream overhead
  • Chinese PLA attack on Taiwan: at hand or too costly to consider?
  • US senators’ Taiwan bill to ‘restructure’ relations with Taipei
  • Explosion at pesticide firm in northwest China leaves at least 8 hurt and 6 missing, cause still unknown
  • China launches Fujian, PLA Navy’s 3rd aircraft carrier
  • New railway completes 2,700km loop of Taklamakan Desert in move to integrate Xinjiang with rest of China
  • China appoints international affairs expert as new consul general in Brisbane
  • China’s beauty slimness obsession highlighted after woman’s weight drops to 25kg in a year with multiple organ failure and refuses treatment
  • China’s youth league to tackle graduate job pressure with advice, aid and a chance to ‘toughen’ up
  • White House adviser calls relationship with India ‘most important for the United States’
  • Why is China’s inflation rate low compared to the US, Europe and Britain?
  • Nicholas Burns, US ambassador to China, stresses need to find common ground amid tense relations
  • US security adviser says hard line on Russia is needed to dissuade China from similar moves
  • More than 90 per cent of oil slicks threatening global marine life caused by humans, US and Chinese scientists find
  • Who can fill the shoes of China’s Iron Lady Sun Chunlan on Politburo?
  • Western-style democracy behind wars, chaos and human misery, Chinese president tells top officials
  • Chinese scientists take ‘important step’ in stem cell therapy to treat Parkinson’s disease
  • US, China economic policies like night and day as Fed pushes interest rate skyward and PBOC eyes stabilisation
  • Taiwan admits paying American lobbyists to help establish closer US ties amid rising pressure from Beijing
  • Amazon, Cartier slap Chinese social media influencer, 8 others with lawsuits over trademark infringement, false advertising
  • China’s top Covid-19 fighter calls for all-in-one data portal like Europe’s EpiPulse
  • China vows private firms will receive ‘equal treatment’ in charm offensive to prop up struggling sector
  • Japan’s tougher cyberbullying laws prompted after Hana Kimura’s suicide sparks free speech debate
  • China-Australia relations: Canberra’s top diplomat Penny Wong visits Solomon Islands to address Beijing security concerns
  • Malaysia’s economic challenge shows how politics has become a bottleneck for growth
  • Thai influencers charged with royal insult over Lazada adverts
  • How J.Y. Pillay helped Lee Kuan Yew turn Singapore Airlines into a global giant
  • South Korea and Japan look to move forward and ‘normalise’ information-sharing by keeping their tense history in the past
  • Indian trains set ablaze in protests against military hiring changes
  • Why the US’ Indo-Pacific Economic Framework is really all about India
  • WTO reaches initial deal as India’s defiance tempered on vaccines, fishing, food security
  • Elimination of nuclear weapons an urgent need, UN chief warns
  • How would a US recession impact Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand amid surging inflation?
  • UK approves US extradition of WikiLeaks’ founder Julian Assange,‘dark day for democracy’
  • Ukrainians threaten to destroy Russian bridge to Crimea ‘target number one’
  • Why Ukraine’s request to join is big test for EU
  • Vladimir Putin’s health: pivotal yet shrouded in uncertainty after reports of treatments
  • Russian spy caught seeking war crimes court internship, Netherlands says
  • WTO strikes landmark deals package after marathon talks, prompting cheers
  • Remembering Vincent Chin, decades after racist killing that was a flash point for Asian-Americans
  • Zelensky hologram references Star Wars: ‘We will defeat the empire too’
  • Omicron less likely to cause long Covid, UK study finds
  • Donald Trump unleashed mob after VP Mike Pence rejected election plot, Capitol riot panel hears
  • Amsterdam’s Schiphol Airport to cut flights over busy summer
  • Can polar bears survive climate change? Scientists see hopeful signs with discovery of new group
  • Kevin Spacey granted bail after ‘strenuously’ denying sex charges
  • Climate Change: Hong Kong well placed to act as go-between in carbon credits market as long as it adopts international standards, experts say
  • St Petersburg bourse to add Alibaba, Tencent, other Hong Kong stocks from June 20 in trial to widen access for Russian investors
  • Tech stocks lift Hong Kong market while Asian equities languish as investors fret about Fed policy outlook, recession
  • Chinese stocks emerge as safe haven from bear markets globally, as US rate rise eclipses Shanghai lockdown as bogeyman
  • Recession and inflation fears give way to a crisis of confidence in economic policymaking
  • HSBC teams up with Emma Raducanu to create new opportunities for young people around the world
  • HSBC leads clients to see beyond the norm with its art initiatives
  • Blockchain gaming unicorn Animoca Brands acquires educational start-up TinyTap to build tokenised learning platform
  • Shanghai reopening: Disneyland ready for visitors once more as theme park awaits green light after Covid-19 lockdown
  • Cheap flights with Kiwi.com saved me US$1,500 but was it worth it? My 41-hour journey from Asia to Europe
  • Cleansing foot pads do not remove toxins from your body, experts say – the foot is not a detoxifying organ
  • She gave away her US$3,000 dream wedding dress on Facebook – now other women are donating their dresses to brides in need
  • How Asia bar industry expert Holly Graham found her calling and why she’s now telling the stories of its people
  • Yoga, meditation with Lululemon at Hong Kong’s M+ museum in summer programme focused on wellness, sustainability and healing
  • Netflix returns to Ghost in the Shell with new Japanese anime series
  • Chinese-American father-son cooks on YouTube channels Made with Lau and Hunger Pangs on how they’ve bonded over food
  • Budapest makeover: how modern architecture is transforming Hungary’s capital
  • Video: Wushu world champion from Hong Kong explains how Chinese kung fu has broadened his horizons
  • Prince William and Kate Middleton’s new ‘normal family home’: Adelaide Cottage is located on royal grounds near Queen Elizabeth and the schools of Princes George and Louis, and Princess Charlotte
  • Hong Kong’s top Father’s Day meal deals, from The Peninsula, W Hong Kong and Agora – spoil dad rotten with Singaporean, Spanish, Cantonese and Japanese flavours
  • 12 best Italian restaurants in Hong Kong, from three-Michelin-starred 8 1/2 Otto e Mezzo Bombana and Ritz-Carlton’s Tosca di Angelo, to Sabatini Ristorante Italiano at the Royal Garden Hotel
  • Meet Lee Jae-wook, star of Netflix K-drama Alchemy of Souls: the 24-year-old rising actor was signed by Hyun Bin’s agency and looks up to Joaquin Phoenix’s The Joker
  • Where is Hong Kong 80s beauty queen May Ng Yuen-fong today? The Miss Hong Kong runner-up married a wealthy business tycoon, lives in a mansion – and hasn’t seemed to age a day
  • Is the real British monarch in Australia? Here’s why some believe Edward IV’s reign was ‘illegitimate’, making farmer Michael Hastings and son Simon the ‘rightful heirs’ instead of Queen Elizabeth
  • Who is Elizabeth Hurley’s son and mirror image, Damian Hurley? The 20-year-old model’s godfathers include Hugh Grant and Elton John, and his dad is late millionaire and property heir, Steve Bing
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 17 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Global stocks in steep weekly retreat after central bank rate rises
  • How fast is the US economy slowing?
  • Bank of England accused of going soft on inflation
  • Covid hospitalisations rise in Europe as sub-variants fuel new wave
  • Stocks still have not reached capitulation point
  • Does the consumer world need to tone down the green talk, for its own good?
  • Live news updates: European Commission supports Ukraine’s bid to join EU
  • Corporate bond funds bleed billions as Fed ramps up inflation fight
  • US home mortgage rates jump by the most since 1987
  • China launches new aircraft carrier in bid to catch up with US capability
  • Deutsche Bank installs app on bankers’ phones to track private messages
  • Air Force One: how Boeing’s prestige project became its albatross
  • The high price society pays for social media
  • Russia’s investment showcase becomes morale-boosting exercise
  • Santander names Héctor Grisi as new chief executive
  • How to split a Big Four firm — and keep 13,000 partners happy
  • Crypto hedge fund Three Arrows fails to meet lender margin calls
  • Crypto’s turbulence teaches hard, but not new, lessons
  • Yen sinks as BoJ sticks to ultra-loose monetary policy
  • Australia invokes emergency powers to block coal exports in energy crisis
  • Musk tells Twitter staffers company must ‘get healthy’
  • Can the Conservatives keep the ‘blue wall’ from crumbling?
  • Macron ally’s election fight underscores threat to president’s majority
  • Rodolfo Hernández’s push for Colombia presidency backed by fervent home town support
  • Inside the Fuggerei, the world’s oldest social housing complex
  • England’s Test win shows the importance of a good leader
  • FirstFT: ‘We are fortunate for Mr Pence’s courage’
  • Afghanistan’s lessons in superpower humility
  • The Bank of Japan will not be bullied
  • The world in our PocketShip
  • Childcare in the UK is a policy mess
  • Russia reduces gas flows to Europe as leaders show support for Ukraine
  • WTO agrees partial patent waiver for Covid-19 vaccines
  • China turns Winter Olympic villages into Covid quarantine camps
  • Should investors step back into emerging markets?
  • Overweight England struggles to break the ‘junk food cycle’
  • Father’s Day advice for new investors
  • Stagflation: one-fifth of European stocks would enter danger zone
  • In charts: e-transport momentum slows in tandem with Covid
  • Trump’s pressure on Pence to overturn 2020 election was illegal, committee hears
  • The Fed’s war on inflation is also a battle for the minds of consumers
  • Democrats have stopped listening to America’s voters
  • The home in 50 objects from around the world #34: the iMac G3 Bondi Blue PC (1998)

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
